Secondary (minor, wording-only) issue: the cell's headline/fact text calls exp(-beta_{n-1}/2)/beta_{n-1} the "exact error term" for the 3/2 ratio. The true exact identity (derivable directly from the recursion, log(1+e^y)=y+log(1+e^{-y})) gives ratio-3/2 = log(1+e^{-beta_{n-1}/2})/beta_{n-1} exactly; exp(-beta_{n-1}/2)/beta_{n-1} is only its leading-order asymptotic approximation (code's own comment correctly calls it "O(e^{-2y})", i.e. approximate) — calling this "exact" in the cell's claims/fact text overstates precision on an otherwise-legitimate asymptotic proof. Net: the individual computations are genuine and reproduce cleanly, but the verdict-determination mechanism as coded cannot actually produce the RESOLVED-B outcome it advertises, which is a material defect in a cell whose entire premise is "obstruction-check gates the reading."
